title: ClientIntake
description: Structured capture of a new client and engagement context. ClientIntake seeds the first-thread assessment workflow (intake -> assessment planning -> discovery -> report -> proposal). It is intended to be consultant-readable, agent-usable, and, in future, groundable by AgentNet. Examples must not contain real client PII.

intake_id.description: Stable local identifier for this intake. Convention: 'intake_' followed by a short slug, unique within Peak's engagement set.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the intake was created.

created_by.description: Anonymized label for the consultant capturing the intake (e.g. 'consultant_a'). No PII in examples.

client_profile.description: High-level profile of the client organization. Names are anonymized in examples.

organization_label.description: Anonymized label for the client organization (e.g. 'client_alpha').

size_indicator.description: Qualitative size indicator rather than a precise figure.

locations_count_indicator.description: Qualitative indicator of number of sites (e.g. 'single', 'few', 'many').

geographies.description: Generic geographic scope (e.g. regions or countries) without identifying a specific client.

operating_model.description: How the client operates its inventory-relevant business (e.g. 'make-to-stock distribution', 'make-to-order manufacturing').

inventory_environment.description: High-level description of the client's inventory environment.

inventory_value_indicator.description: Qualitative indicator of inventory value materiality (e.g. 'low', 'moderate', 'high').

known_systems.description: Systems the client is known to use for inventory-relevant work. Described generically to avoid vendor lock-in framing.

role.description: What the system is used for in the inventory process.

stated_pain_points.description: Pain points the client has stated, in the client's own framing.

evidence_references.description: EvidenceReference ids supporting this pain point, where available.

stakeholders.description: Stakeholders or roles relevant to the engagement. Anonymized in examples.

anonymized_label.description: Anonymized reference label for this stakeholder (e.g. 'stakeholder_1').

involvement.description: Nature/degree of involvement in the engagement.

urgency.description: Why now — the business trigger and its urgency.

business_trigger.description: The event or condition prompting the engagement (e.g. 'failed audit', 'stockouts', 'growth').

available_data_sources.description: Data sources the client may make available for the assessment.

name.description: Data source name or generic label.

initial_scope_hypothesis.description: The consultant's early hypothesis about where the assessment should focus. Explicitly a hypothesis, not a conclusion.

first_billing_tranche_objective.description: The commercial objective of the first billing tranche (the initial assessment), phrased so it can inform a later next-phase proposal.

assessment_readiness.description: How ready the engagement is to proceed to assessment.

blockers.description: Known blockers to starting the assessment.

evidence_references.description: EvidenceReference ids supporting this intake overall.

consultant_notes.description: Free-text consultant notes about this intake.
